Epigenetic Mechanism Underlying the Development of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)-Like Phenotypes in Prenatally Androgenized Rhesus Monkeys

evidence_entities.text: PCOS-like phenotypes are produced by prenatal androgenization (PA) of female rhesus monkeys
perturbation of the epigenome, through altered DNA methylation, is one of the mechanisms whereby PA reprograms monkeys to develop PCOS
differentially methylated loci between PA and control monkeys, 163 in infant VAT, and 325 in adult VAT
significant pathways, including the antiproliferative role of TOB in T cell signaling and transforming growth factor-β (TGF-β) signaling
alteration of the epigenome is a mechanism whereby gestational androgen excess, or its consequences, may reprogram fetal female monkeys to develop PCOS-like traits in adulthood
